title: Lagos Property Buyers Face Higher Costs Following Revised Land Valuation Policy
description: Lagos Property Buyers Face Higher Costs Following Revised Land Valuation Policy  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: Lagos Raises Land Transaction Fees as New Property Valuation Sparks Up to 300% Increase housinglatest local news May 17 Written By Ayomide Fiyinfunoluwa Lagos Real Estate Sector Reacts as Land Transaction Charges Surge The Lagos State Government has introduced a revised property valuation framework that significantly increases statutory land transaction charges across the state, with some fees projected to rise by as much as 300% in high-value real estate districts. The adjustment follows the implementation of the 2026 Fair Market Value (FMV) framework, commonly referred to as the “Blue Book,” which took effect on May 1, 2026. / You Might Also Like / The 2026 Dangote Refinery Blueprint: Location, IPO, and Global Energy Impact The Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway: Transforming Nigeria’s Infrastructure Landscape Nigeria’s Housing Deficit: How Big Is the Problem in 2026? Mistakes First-Time Property Investors Make in Nigeria The revised framework affects several statutory property charges, including Governor’s Consent fees, Stamp Duties, Registration fees, Capital Contribution Levies, Charting fees and other land-related payments tied to property transactions in Lagos. State authorities say the changes are intended to align official property valuations with current market realities after years of rapid appreciation in land values across the state. Revised Valuation Framework Reshapes Property Costs According to information released by the Lagos State Lands Bureau and reports from industry stakeholders, the updated valuation structure recalibrates official property values used to determine statutory fees payable during land transactions. The framework replaces valuation benchmarks that had remained largely unchanged between 2005 and 2015 despite substantial growth in Lagos property prices. Under the revised system, transaction-related costs in prime districts such as Lekki, Ikoyi and Banana Island are expected to rise sharply due to higher assessed property values. In Lekki Phase 1, Governor’s Consent fees that previously ranged between ₦12 million and ₦18 million under earlier valuation benchmarks are now projected to increase to between ₦40 million and ₦90 million. The adjustment reflects updated land valuations estimated between ₦800 million and ₦1.5 billion, compared to approximately ₦250 million under the previous framework. Similarly, properties in Ikoyi that previously attracted transaction perfection costs of between ₦25 million and ₦40 million could now incur fees ranging from ₦100 million to ₦250 million. Officials attribute the increase to revised land values now estimated between ₦2 billion and ₦4 billion in some locations. For high-end waterfront properties in Banana Island, transaction perfection costs may now range between ₦700 million and ₦1 billion, based on property values estimated at approximately ₦10 billion. Government Defends Policy as Market Alignment Measure The Lagos State Government maintains that the revised charges are not s
summary: The revised land valuation policy in Lagos is expected to significantly increase statutory land transaction charges, potentially impacting property buyers and developers, and may affect Mixta Africa's revenue targets and pricing strategies.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: The increased land transaction charges may impact Mixta Africa's revenue targets, particularly for projects like Lakowe Crossings and Lakowe Annexe, which are focused on the mid-market and premium segments.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The revised policy may also affect Mixta Africa's pricing strategies, potentially leading to higher costs for buyers and reduced demand for affordable housing products.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The policy change may create an opportunity for Mixta Africa to re-evaluate its product mix and pricing strategies to mitigate the impact of increased costs and capitalize on the growing demand for premium and mid-market products.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The increased land transaction charges may pose a risk to Mixta Africa's execution of its strategic priorities, particularly in terms of recovering concentrated receivables and achieving its revenue targets.

title: Lagos Govt Engages Stakeholders On Land Reforms, Estate Management
description: Lagos Govt Engages Stakeholders On Land Reforms, Estate Management  Independent Newspaper Nigeria
content: News Lagos Govt Engages Stakeholders On Land Reforms, Estate Management by Victoria Ojugbana April 28, 2026 written by Victoria Ojugbana April 28, 2026 Share 0FacebookTwitterPinterestEmail Discover more The Lagos State government has said it will continue to seek stakeholders’ input in land reforms, sales of landed property and estate management in the state to ensure that premium prices are realised in the sale of landed properties in the state. Permanent Secretary, Lands Bureau, Kamar Olowosago, disclosed this during stakeholders’ engagement on Fair Market Value FMV (Blue Book) held at Alausa, Ikeja, on Tuesday. He noted that the stakeholders review of the Fair Market Value (FMV) framework for land administration was aimed at improving transparency, attracting investments and strengthening the state’s revenue base. At the launch of the newly-reviewed book, he stated that the expected outcomes of FMV include boosting real estate development, building a more accurate land database, and improving Lagos’ Ease of Doing Business ranking and global competitiveness, and increase Internally Generated Revenue (IGR) for the state. According to Olowosago, the FMV policy was first conceived in 2012 after authorities observed that rates used for land-related charges had remained unchanged since 2005 and no longer reflected prevailing market realities. The Permanent Secretary said the Lagos State subsequently synergized with the Nigerian Institution of Estate Surveyors and Valuers to compile updated market values across locations in Lagos, leading to the institutionalisation of a new valuation regime through Executive Order No. EO/BRF/001 of 2015 signed by former Governor Babatunde Fashola. Saying that the framework was later gazetted, giving it full legal backing, Olowosago added that the main objectives of the reform were to reduce the cost of land transactions, improve ease of doing business, ensure transparency and align government charges with actual market values. Explaining that the policy also provides for periodic reviews every five years to keep pace with urban expansion and market changes, the Permanent Secretary added a review was carried out in 2021 under Governor Babajide Sanwo-Olu’s administration, while another cycle has commenced. Olowosago said the latest review is anchored on four strategic pillars, including ease of doing business, revenue optimisation, transparency and market alignment. He added that the exercise would capture previously omitted and emerging areas, improve neighbourhood classification, ensure fairness in valuation and strengthen the integrity of the land administration system. Olowosago, who allayed fears that the review could lead to higher transaction costs, added that the process was designed to ensure fairness rather than impose additional burdens. “A transparent and market-aligned system reduces disputes, shortens transaction timelines, enhances investor confidence and creates a level playing field,” Olowosago said.
summary: The Lagos State government's engagement with stakeholders on land reforms and estate management is expected to boost transparency, attract investments, and strengthen the state's revenue base, ultimately driving up premium prices for landed properties in Lagos.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This initiative may indirectly impact our strategic priority of shifting new-sales mix toward higher-margin segments, as increased transparency and premium prices may favor our premium Lakowe Lakes products.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The initiative may also impact our costs and pricing, as we may need to adjust our pricing strategy to reflect the new market dynamics.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: This creates an opportunity for Mixta Africa to leverage the increased transparency and premium prices to attract more institutional and bulk transactions, and corporate mortgage programmes.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: There is a risk that the increased costs and pricing may impact our ability to meet our revenue targets, particularly if we are unable to pass on the increased costs to our customers.

title: From Concrete to Confidence: How Nigeria can rebuild trust in its real estate development industry
description: From Concrete to Confidence: How Nigeria can rebuild trust in its real estate development industry  Business News Nigeria
content: Share “The hardest thing to build in Nigerian real estate today is credibility.” Nigeria’s real estate sector has the foundations of a compelling market. Demand is strong, the population continues to grow, and the appetite for quality housing, from both local and diaspora buyers, remains high. Yet one question continues to shape how buyers and investors engage with the market. Can this developer be trusted? That concern has moved beyond location or design. Buyers want to know if a project will be delivered, at the promised quality, and within a reasonable timeframe. The idea of rebuilding is deliberate. There was a time when a handshake from the right developer carried weight, when off-plan purchases were made on the strength of reputation, and when commitments were honoured. That trust did not disappear overnight, it weakened over time through missed timelines, inconsistent standards, and limited accountability. Nigeria’s housing deficit is significant, but the more immediate challenge is the trust deficit. It shapes how capital flows and how buyers make decisions. Until it is addressed, even credible developers will continue to operate in an environment where confidence has to be earned repeatedly. The Reality of Building in Today’s Market Anyone building in this market knows the economic realities first-hand. Inflation and exchange rate volatility have had a real impact on development costs across the country. Between groundbreaking and completion, the cost of materials, especially those tied to foreign exchange, can change significantly. With the off-plan model, units are often sold at earlier prices while costs continue to rise during construction. These are challenges developers deal with regularly, but they are not an excuse for poor delivery. If anything, they call for more discipline and better planning. We saw this play out on one of our projects in Abuja. We started with a clear sourcing plan, but COVID disrupted supply chains almost overnight. Materials scheduled for importation became difficult to access, and at the same time, exchange rates moved sharply, pushing costs beyond what we had anticipated. From that experience, we learned that managing foreign exchange exposure has to be intentional. Where materials need to be imported, timing matters. Moving early to lock in costs can help reduce exposure to fluctuations. Where possible, we also look at local alternatives without compromising on quality. In some cases, engaging contractors ahead of milestones helps to stabilise pricing. It is not foolproof, but it is a necessary part of managing risk. This is simply the nature of the business. External shocks, whether economic or social, can affect a project midstream. The goal is to anticipate those risks, plan ahead, and manage them in a way that keeps the project on track. Financing, Investment, and Long-Term Value In Nigeria, inflation is not just a cost issue, it is also a financing issue. The cost of capital is high, so how a proje
summary: The erosion of trust in Nigeria's real estate development industry poses a significant challenge to the Lagos market, particularly for Mixta Africa, as it threatens to undermine the company's strategic priorities and active projects, including Lakowe Crossings and Lagos New Town.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: This article highlights the need to rebuild trust in Nigeria's real estate development industry, which directly affects Mixta Africa's strategic priority of recovering concentrated receivables and shifting new-sales mix toward higher-margin segments.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The erosion of trust may lead to increased costs and regulatory hurdles for Mixta Africa, affecting the pricing and costs of its projects.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The article presents an opportunity for Mixta Africa to differentiate itself by prioritizing project delivery, quality, and accountability, thereby rebuilding trust with buyers and investors.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The loss of trust in the industry poses a significant risk to Mixta Africa's execution, particularly if the company fails to address the concerns raised in the article.

title: Minimalist & Co unveils M120 Apartments, setting new benchmark for lifestyle living in Lekki
description: Minimalist & Co unveils M120 Apartments, setting new benchmark for lifestyle living in Lekki  The Guardian Nigeria News
content: Minimalist & Co unveils M120 Apartments, setting new benchmark for lifestyle living in Lekki By : Victor Gbonegun Date: 10 February 2026 4:36pm WAT Share : Follow Us on Google Discover Exterior view of M120 Apartments, a lifestyle-led residential development by Minimalist & Co, located off Freedom Way in Lekki Phase 1. Exterior view of M120 Apartments, a lifestyle-led residential development by Minimalist & Co, located off Freedom Way in Lekki Phase 1. As Lagos continues to struggle with rising congestion, long commute times, and increasing demand for high-quality residential spaces, real estate developer Minimalist & Co has unveiled M120 Apartments, a premium lifestyle-led residential community designed to address the realities of modern urban living. The launch event, held in Lekki, brought together real estate investors, prospective homeowners, industry professionals, and business leaders for an on-site experience of the development. Guests were taken through guided tours of the property and its amenities, offering a firsthand look at their design philosophy and living experience behind the project. Speaking at the event, the Managing Partner of Minimalist & Co, Mr Dolapo Adebayo, described the project as the result of a deliberate response to how people live in the city. “M120 was conceived to solve a clear problem we see in Lagos today. People spend too much time commuting just to access basic lifestyle needs. We wanted to create a development where living, working, unwinding, and staying active could happen in one place. This launch represents the outcome of years of focused planning and execution. Quality was our highest priority throughout the development process, and we did not compromise on it at any stage. Looking ahead, our focus remains on delivering lifestyle-driven developments that hold value over time and set a new standard for residential living,” he said. Related News Why water-efficient houseplants are becoming the future of urban living Sanwo-Olu, Nola break ground for Veritasi Homes, COOPLAG 22-floor Oyster Towers in Eko Atlantic City Solving high construction costs in Nigeria M120 Apartments is a multi-level residential development offering 2-bedroom apartments, 3-bedroom apartments, and 4-bedroom Penthouse units. The project was designed to support modern urban living by integrating everyday conveniences and lifestyle amenities within the estate. The development features what the company describes as Lekki’s first padel court within a residential estate, alongside a swimming pool for adults and children, a fully equipped gym, landscaped green areas, a children’s playground, a cafe designed for remote work and an on-site supermarket. The launch represents the outcome of years of focused planning and execution by Minimalist & Co. The event was hosted by Comedian and Media Personality Bovi, who highlighted the project’s investment performance, noting that the development had recorded over 200% appreciation since inception. He
summary: The launch of M120 Apartments by Minimalist & Co sets a new benchmark for lifestyle living in Lekki, potentially increasing competition for premium residential developments in the area. This may impact Mixta Africa's strategic priorities, particularly in the premium segment. The development's focus on lifestyle-led residential community could influence buyer preferences and expectations in the Lagos real estate market.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The launch of M120 Apartments may affect pricing and buyer expectations in the premium segment, potentially influencing sales of Mixta Africa's premium products, such as Lakowe Lakes.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The focus on lifestyle-led residential communities could create an opportunity for Mixta Africa to enhance its own premium offerings and differentiate them in the market.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Increased competition in the premium segment could pose a risk to Mixta Africa's sales and revenue targets if not addressed through strategic marketing and product differentiation.

title: A Reawakening For Global Listed Real Estate
description: A Reawakening For Global Listed Real Estate  Wealth Briefing
content: Print (subject to copyright) Request Reprint Rights This article argues that global listed real estate is due for a recovery, and that certain regions, such as Asia and Europe, are faring particularly well. The following article is by Rogier Quirijns (pictured below), head of European real estate at Cohen & Steers, an international investment firm headquartered in New York. The article looks at the listed property market, such as real estate investment trusts (REITs) and what he considers to be the opportunities – and certain risks – in the space. The editors are pleased to share these views; the usual editorial disclaimers apply to views of guest writers. To comment, email tom.burroughes@wealthbriefing.com and amanda.cheesley@clearviewpublishing.com Rogier Quirijns After years of US dominance, global real estate is staging a comeback – reshaping investor expectations and allocations. International REITs are outperforming the US for the first time since 2017, led by Asia and Europe. Discounted valuations, healthy supply and demand, maturing sectors, and supportive policies are driving global real estate’s strong rebound, reinforcing why diversification is essential amid varied macro trends and secular growth drivers. A global comeback years in the making Global real estate is on pace to outperform US real estate for the first time since 2017. Through the third quarter of 2025, global REITs were up 10.4 per cent, compared with US REITs, which went up 4.5 per cent over the same period. What is notable, given that US markets account for over 60 per cent of global real estate, is how strongly Asia-Pacific, Europe, and emerging markets have performed. Asia-Pacific leads with a 27.4 per cent gain, followed by Europe at 17.9 per cent and emerging markets at 16.2 per cent. This is in stark contrast to recent history, when the US served as a safe haven while Asia grappled with political turmoil and Europe struggled with slower growth. Over the past five years, US REITs returned 7 per cent annually, while Europe and emerging markets had negative annual returns, and Asia posted just 3.5 per cent returns. 2025 marked a reversal of recent trends. China, which returned nearly 30 per cent on indications growth, has bottomed, and Japan, Spain, Hong Kong, and the Netherlands have all posted returns above 20 per cent. Importantly, in our view the second half of the 2020s will likely look more like previous decades, with longer-term returns driven by fundamentals, which remain healthy. Historically, US listed real estate annual returns dating back to 1991 show that fundamentals – not sentiment – anchor multi-year outcomes. From discounted to delivering Favourable valuations, improving fundamentals, and a positive macroeconomic backdrop are driving the comeback in international markets. Europe and Asia were trading at glaringly discounted valuations at the start of 2025. US REITs began the year trading at a slight premium to net asset values; by comparison, Europe a
summary: The global listed real estate market's recovery, particularly in Asia and Europe, may influence investor expectations and allocations, potentially benefiting Lagos' premium and mid-market segments as international investors seek opportunities in emerging markets.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: May influence our ability to attract institutional investors for bulk transactions and corporate mortgage programmes, including the MREIF/MOFI diaspora and domestic mortgage partnership with Access Bank.

mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Creates an opportunity to scale Lagos New Town (LNT) and attract international investors to our premium and mid-market segments.

title: Lagos–Calabar Coastal Road Links Infrastructure Growth to Rising Coastal Property Values
description: Lagos–Calabar Coastal Road Links Infrastructure Growth to Rising Coastal Property Values  THISDAYLIVE
content: Friday, 19th June, 2026 Home Business Politics Nigeria International Africa Rest of the World Health & Wellbeing Education Life & Style Sport Editorial Backpage Breaking News Govs Demand Constitutional Safeguards for State Police 1 day ago UK Court Clears Ex-Minister, Alison-Madueke, of All Graft Charges 1 day ago Eyesan Highlights Infrastructure, Asset Integrity as Key Constraints in Oil Industry 1 day ago INEC: 1,059,360 Registered Voters to Elect New Governor for Ekiti State June 20 1 day ago Latest Headlines Odukoya Memorial Lecture to Focus on Nigeria’s Leadership Crisis Sanwo-Olu Felicitates Petralon Energy Boss, Unuigbe, at 50 Abia, UNICEF, ILO Target Functional Social Register NDC Candidate, Gwarzo, Questions Kano’s Evidence in Alleged LG Funds Diversion Scandal Lagos–Calabar Coastal Road Links Infrastructure Growth to Rising Coastal Property Values Business | 5 months ago Shares Share Post Email Share Share Raheem Akingbolu The Lagos–Calabar Coastal Road corridor, from Eko Atlantic City to the Bluewater Okunde zone, is drawing attention from investors tracking shifts in Nigeria’s property market. Market data and project activity indicate that the corridor is influencing land values, investment timing, and development patterns along the Lagos coastline. The road, which links Eko Atlantic City with coastal districts to the east, is being viewed as “more than a transport artery” by market participants. Its role in improving access between Victoria Island, Eko Atlantic City and Bluewater Okunde has changed how developers and investors assess the coastline. Industry sources say the combination of public infrastructure delivery, zoning direction and private capital is shaping a defined investment belt. Developments along the route include high-rise residential projects such as The Carnelian on Victoria Island, alongside planned schemes in Bluewater Okunde. These projects are being marketed to investors seeking entry before construction completion. Analysts note that infrastructure-led corridors often experience price shifts once access and planning certainty improve. Land pricing along the Bluewater Okunde strip illustrates this trend. A parcel measuring about 2,400 square metres, which traded at around ₦800 million in earlier market cycles, is now valued above ₦5 billion, according to market estimates. Analysts link the change to road access, proximity to Eko Atlantic City, zoning clarity, ocean frontage and supporting services. The coastal road now provides direct movement between Victoria Island, Eko Atlantic City and Bluewater Okunde, altering development timelines and exit strategies. Bluewater Okunde is increasingly described as an extension of Eko Atlantic City’s real estate market. The zone sits within a short drive of Eko Atlantic and within reach of Victoria Island’s business district. Market observers compare its current phase with early stages of waterfront districts in Dubai Marina, Miami Beach and Canary Wharf, citing infrastruct
summary: The development of the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road is expected to drive growth in coastal property values, positively impacting the Lagos real estate market, particularly in areas like Lekki and Ibeju-Lekki. This infrastructure project could enhance the attractiveness of Mixta Africa's premium products, such as those in Lakowe Lakes. As a result, the company may experience increased demand and potentially higher sales revenues.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: The project's impact on coastal property values could directly benefit Mixta Africa's premium developments, such as Lakowe Lakes, by increasing their attractiveness and potential resale value.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The improved infrastructure may lead to increased demand for housing and commercial properties in the region, potentially driving up prices and benefiting Mixta Africa's business.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The development of the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Road presents an opportunity for Mixta Africa to focus on premium and commercial developments, aligning with its strategic priority to shift the new-sales mix toward higher-margin segments.

title: Police Probe $250,000 Loan, Lekki Property Dispute as Key Parties Ignore Summons
description: Police Probe $250,000 Loan, Lekki Property Dispute as Key Parties Ignore Summons  THISDAYLIVE
content: Friday, 19th June, 2026 Home Business Politics Nigeria International Africa Rest of the World Health & Wellbeing Education Life & Style Sport Editorial Backpage Breaking News Govs Demand Constitutional Safeguards for State Police 1 day ago UK Court Clears Ex-Minister, Alison-Madueke, of All Graft Charges 1 day ago Eyesan Highlights Infrastructure, Asset Integrity as Key Constraints in Oil Industry 1 day ago INEC: 1,059,360 Registered Voters to Elect New Governor for Ekiti State June 20 1 day ago Latest Headlines Odukoya Memorial Lecture to Focus on Nigeria’s Leadership Crisis Sanwo-Olu Felicitates Petralon Energy Boss, Unuigbe, at 50 Abia, UNICEF, ILO Target Functional Social Register NDC Candidate, Gwarzo, Questions Kano’s Evidence in Alleged LG Funds Diversion Scandal Police Probe $250,000 Loan, Lekki Property Dispute as Key Parties Ignore Summons Nigeria | 5 months ago Shares Share Post Email Share Share Wale Igbintade The failure of key parties to honour police invitations has become a central issue in an ongoing investigation into a disputed $250,000 loan claim and competing interests over a prime Lekki property in Lagos. Police sources confirmed that despite formal invitations issued as part of the investigation, Ms. Memunat Ajoke Olubando and a former senator, Domingo Obende, have yet to present themselves for questioning several months after the matter was formally reported to law enforcement authorities. The investigation followed a petition submitted by businessman and industrialist, Chief Razaq Okoya, to the Inspector-General of Police and the Lagos State Commissioner of Police. The petition was filed on behalf of Chief Okoya, his wife, Chief (Mrs.) Shade Okoya, and their company, RAO Investment Property Company Limited. At the centre of the dispute is a claim by Senator Obende that he advanced a $250,000 loan, allegedly secured with a high-value Lekki property linked to Chief Okoya. The petitioners have, however, categorically denied the existence of any such loan arrangement or collateral agreement. Sources close to Chief Okoya insist that he has no record of any personal or business dealings with Senator Obende and that neither he nor any of his companies obtained a loan from the former lawmaker. They also dispute claims that any property belonging to Chief Okoya or his company was used as security for a loan. According to the petition, concerns were raised over documents submitted to the Lagos State Lands Registry in connection with the disputed property. The petition requested the police to examine the circumstances surrounding the handling of the property’s Certificate of Occupancy and to determine whether due process was followed. The matter was initially reported to the Lagos State Commissioner of Police through a petition authored by the late legal practitioner, Hon. (Chief) Bankole Oki. Follow-up petitions were subsequently submitted in October 2025, calling for a comprehensive investigation into the loan claim and related pr
summary: The police probe into a $250,000 loan and Lekki property dispute may create uncertainty in the Lagos real estate market, potentially affecting Mixta Africa's premium and affordable housing segments.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: The dispute may impact our premium Lakowe Lakes products and LNT commercial plots, which are key to our strategy of shifting new-sales mix toward higher-margin segments.

mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The risk of execution delays or increased regulatory scrutiny may threaten our ability to meet our revenue targets and execute our strategic priorities.

title: Saheed Osupa commits ₦2bn to landmark housing scheme for fans in Ibeju-Lekki
description: Saheed Osupa commits ₦2bn to landmark housing scheme for fans in Ibeju-Lekki  PM News Nigeria
content: News Published By Michael Adesina Date 24th January, 2026 SHARE: Saheed Osupa Quick Read Legendary Fuji music icon and cultural ambassador, King Saheed Osupa, has committed ₦2 billion to a landmark housing initiative aimed at expanding homeownership opportunities for his fans in Ibeju-Lekki, Lagos State. Tolulope Oke Legendary Fuji music icon and cultural ambassador, King Saheed Osupa, has committed ₦2 billion to a landmark housing initiative aimed at expanding homeownership opportunities for his fans in Ibeju-Lekki, Lagos State. The project follows his acquisition of 50 hectares of land from Harmony Gardens & Estate Development Ltd, where a new residential estate, Osupa Boulevard, will be developed within Harmony Casa 2.0, one of the fast-emerging property corridors in the Lekki axis. Designed as a fan-focused housing scheme, Osupa Boulevard is expected to deliver about 300 housing units and serviced plots, offering subscribers flexible ownership options made possible through a strategic partnership with the Ibile Traditional Mortgage System. According to his management, the initiative as one of the most ambitious celebrity-driven housing interventions in Nigeria, particularly at a time when demand for land and property in Ibeju-Lekki continues to surge due to major infrastructure projects, including the Lekki Free Trade Zone, Dangote Refinery, and other industrial developments. The musician’s management also confirmed that Saheed Osupa committed a ₦2 billion payment guarantee on behalf of his fans, enabling immediate land allocation by Harmony Gardens once subscribers make their initial deposits. The management added that interested buyers can secure plots with an initial payment of ₦5 million, begin construction immediately, and spread the balance over three to six months. The initiative is designed to remove common barriers faced by musicians, creatives, and fans who often struggle with access to credible real estate opportunities and flexible financing structures. Beyond land ownership, the Fuji icon also entered into a second agreement with Harmony Gardens to provide ready-built housing options within the estate. Under this plan, subscribers can purchase completed homes and spread payments over a five-year period, further easing access to homeownership. Confirming the development in a statement shared on his Instagram page, Saheed Osupa described the project as both an investment opportunity and a legacy initiative for his fans. “I’m proud to officially announce my partnership with the Ibile Traditional Mortgage System to bring unprecedented opportunities in real estate investment. For the next six months, my fans worldwide can access exclusive discounts on prime lands and properties, including the prestigious Osupa Boulevard at Harmony Gardens,” he stated. According to him, the project goes beyond commercial interest, offering fans a chance to build lasting wealth and secure their future through property ownership. Harmony Gardens & Estate
summary: The commitment of ₦2 billion by Saheed Osupa to a landmark housing initiative in Ibeju-Lekki, Lagos State, is expected to expand homeownership opportunities for his fans, potentially increasing demand for affordable housing in the region. This development may impact the pricing strategy of similar projects in the area, including Mixta Africa's active projects. The project's focus on affordable housing could also influence the market's perception of the viability of such projects in the region.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Potential impact on pricing strategy for similar projects, including Lakowe Crossings and Lagos New Town
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Opportunity to explore partnerships or collaborations with influential figures like Saheed Osupa to expand Mixta Africa's reach in the affordable housing segment
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Risk of increased competition in the affordable housing segment, potentially affecting sales of Mixta Africa's projects

title: Lekki waterfront property: Investors urged to exercise extreme caution
description: Lekki waterfront property: Investors urged to exercise extreme caution  Tribune Online
content: Enjoy our articles? Support us on Google: Add as a preferred source INVESTORS and members of the public have been urged to exercise caution in any dealings on the disputed Lekki waterfront property, Lagos. According to property buyer, Mr Henry Ugonna Orabuchi, the advice become imperative following ongoing civil and criminal proceedings on the property. He said the matter is before the courts, adding that no amount of propaganda can alter the facts. He maintained that he is the lawful purchaser of 3,000 square metres of reclaimed waterfront land, adding the he also fulfilled all financial obligations under the contract. He added that, all documents, agreements, payment records and regulatory reports in regards to the property are before courts of competent jurisdiction. He clarified circumstances surrounding the recent sealing of a property, countering what he described as misleading online reports that suggested a wrongful shutdown of unrelated business premises. Orabuchi stated that he entered into a land transaction in 2022 for the purchase of reclaimed waterfront land, following representations that the land would be sand-filled and sold under a valid root of title. He said that he made several payments during the course of the transaction and was shown documents, including a Lagos State Certificate of Occupancy covering the adjoining land, with assurances that the reclaimed portion would derive consent from the same title. Access to the waterfront portion, he said, was also agreed to be through the adjoining developed property. Four Anambra lawmakers dump Labour Party for ADC However, he explained that disagreements later arose over regulatory assessments, documentation, and the extent of land recognised by relevant authorities, following interventions by Lagos State agencies. These developments, according to him, led to disputes over title perfection and compliance with planning regulations. summary: The disputed Lekki waterfront property issue may lead to increased caution among investors in the Lagos real estate market, potentially affecting demand and pricing in the premium and commercial segments. This development could have a medium-term impact on the market, particularly in the Lekki area. As a result, developers like Mixta Africa may need to reassess their strategies and prioritize transparency to maintain investor confidence.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Potential impact on pricing and demand in the premium segment, which may affect the sales of Mixta Africa's Lakowe Lakes products
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: Increased risk of reputational damage if Mixta Africa is perceived as being involved in disputed or problematic projects

title: 2026 commercial real estate outlook
content: 6 min read Browse by topic Real Estate Commercial Real Estate Commercial Term Lending Community Development Banking Multifamily 1/5 The 2026 commercial real estate outlook is bright. Multifamily and industrial remain strong, retail is steady and office is bouncing back in some metropolitan areas. “In 2025, we saw improvements in real estate equity fundraising and transaction volume,” said Michelle Herrick, Head of Commercial Real Estate at J.P. Morgan. “The 2026 market is strong from both a capital and fundamental standpoint—we anticipate more transactions in the coming year.” We pride ourselves on providing clients with through-the-cycle reliability regardless of the economic climate. Connect with a banker Macroeconomic and geopolitical trends impacting CRE The 43-day federal government shutdown consumed headlines for much of the fall, significantly impacting the economy and, in turn, commercial real estate. The shutdown primarily impacted community development real estate, which relies heavily on government funding and resources, with Community Development Financial Institutions (CDFIs) and Community Development Entities seeing the greatest effects. The shutdown and its impacts on housing programs present new challenges for CDFIs and the communities they serve. While the administration rescinded CDFI Fund layoffs made during the shutdown, the Office of Management and Budget (OMB) is withholding already appropriated CDFI Fund money. These changes, among others may delay new projects and investments. The continuing resolution passed Nov. 12 extends FY2025 government appropriations through Jan. 30, 2026. Another federal shutdown could be on the horizon. “That could hamper dealmaking activity through reduced investor confidence and demand, as well as elevated economic and political uncertainty,” said Ginger Chambless, Head of Market Insights for Commercial Banking at J.P. Morgan. Beyond the shutdown, federal policies on regulation, immigration, and inflation and interest rates—and the uncertainty around them all—remain top of mind for investors. Tariffs: “Tariff changes have already impacted building material costs,” Chambless said. For example, steel, aluminum and copper parts are all subject to a 50% tariff. Other building material costs could also be subject to finalized reciprocal rates and trade agreements with varying trade partners. Interest rates and inflation: “What has slowed down construction in multifamily, for example, hasn't solely been tariffs,” said Kurt Stuart, Co-Head of Commercial Term Lending at Chase. “It has been interest rates.” Despite modest upward price pressure on core goods inflation, and overall inflation above the Fed’s 2% target, in late October the Fed announced it would end its quantitative tightening measures starting Dec. 1. Immigration: “Immigration will be an important area to monitor as labor costs are also a substantial portion of commercial real estate project costs,” Chambless said. Reduced labor supply coul
summary: The 2026 commercial real estate outlook is bright, with multifamily and industrial remaining strong, retail steady, and office bouncing back in some metropolitan areas, which is a positive indicator for Lagos' commercial real estate market, particularly for Mixta Africa's Lakowe Lakes and LNT commercial plots.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: The positive commercial real estate outlook supports Mixta Africa's strategic priorities of shifting new-sales mix toward higher-margin segments, such as Lakowe Lakes products and LNT commercial plots.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The improving office market may lead to increased demand for commercial spaces in Lagos, potentially driving up prices and costs for Mixta Africa's projects.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The strong demand for commercial spaces creates a strategic opportunity for Mixta Africa to pursue institutional/bulk transactions and corporate mortgage programmes.

title: China launches commercial property REITs pilot to back new real estate model - China Daily
description: China launches commercial property REITs pilot to back new real estate model  China Daily - Global Edition
content: By Zhou Lanxu | chinadaily.com.cn | Updated: 2025-12-31 21:50 China has launched a pilot program for commercial property real estate investment trusts (REITs), a key step toward using market-based mechanisms to support a new model of real estate development and promote innovation in asset securitization. On Wednesday, the China Securities Regulatory Commission (CSRC) released an announcement on rolling out the commercial property REITs pilot, taking effect immediately, with the Shanghai and Shenzhen stock exchanges releasing supporting rule sets. With the framework now in place, market participants can submit applications to the CSRC as well as the exchanges. Commercial property REITs are defined as closed-end publicly offered securities investment funds that hold commercial real estate to generate stable cash flows and distribute returns to fund holders. The move is seen as China's latest step to support the building of a new development model of the real estate sector — by activating existing assets, mitigating financial risks in the sector and raising the share of direct financing — while meeting residents' wealth management needs. According to the announcement, commercial properties to be held by REITs must align with national strategies, development plans and industrial policies, have clear ownership, a well-defined scope, complete key compliance procedures, and a track record of sustained, stable cash flows. Supporting rules from the exchanges specify that proceeds from REITs issuance should not be used to purchase land for commercial residential housing. They also introduce market-oriented return requirements linked to the yield on 10-year government bonds. Sources said the initial phase of the pilot will proceed prudently, prioritizing quality over speed and maintaining strict standards for issuance, compliance and risk control. Priority will be given to high-quality, representative projects aligned with national policy goals, including commercial complexes, retail properties, office buildings and hotels. Project reserves are considered relatively ample, with overall quality assessed as solid, the sources said. The launch of the pilot marks a new phase for China's REITs market, with commercial property and infrastructure REITs developing in parallel, moving toward a more comprehensive and mature market covering a broader range of assets. The CSRC also said it will expand REITs-related indices, support eligible public funds in including REITs in their investments, and explore innovative products such as exchange-traded funds tracking REITs indices to broaden investors' asset allocation options.
summary: The launch of commercial property REITs in China may have a long-term impact on the Lagos real estate market by introducing new investment models and potentially increasing foreign investment. This could lead to increased competition in the premium and commercial segments. However, the immediate impact on the Lagos market is expected to be negligible.

mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: Potential increase in foreign investment and new investment models may affect pricing and costs in the premium and commercial segments
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: Mixta Africa could explore partnerships or investments in REITs to diversify its portfolio and increase its competitiveness

title: Global Real Estate Outlook
summary: The global real estate outlook suggests a shift towards sustainable and resilient development, which may favor Mixta Africa's premium Lakowe Lakes products and LNT commercial plots, but also poses a risk to our affordable housing segment due to increased competition from institutional investors.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: The shift towards sustainable development may create opportunities for Mixta Africa to differentiate its premium products, but also poses a risk to our affordable housing segment if we fail to adapt to changing market conditions.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The increased competition from institutional investors may lead to higher costs and pricing pressures for Mixta Africa's affordable housing segment.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The focus on sustainable development creates an opportunity for Mixta Africa to position itself as a leader in environmentally friendly and resilient development, which may attract premium buyers and institutional investors.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: The increased competition from institutional investors poses a risk to Mixta Africa's execution of its strategic priorities, particularly in the affordable housing segment.

title: Nigeria’s Real Estate Market Reaches ₦41.3 Trillion, Highlighting Investment Opportunities
description: Nigeria’s Real Estate Market Reaches ₦41.3 Trillion, Highlighting Investment Opportunities  nigeriahousingmarket.com
content: Nigeria’s Real Estate Sector Hits ₦41.3 Trillion, Unlocking New Investment Horizons daily Dec 1 Written By Amarachi okeke Nigeria’s Real Estate Market Reaches ₦41.3 Trillion, Highlighting Investment Opportunities Nigeria’s real estate sector has reached an estimated ₦41.3 trillion market value, reinforcing its position as a key frontier for long-term investment. This growth is driven by rapid urbanisation, a rising population, and persistent housing shortages, creating significant opportunities for investors with strategic vision and patient capital. Industry data indicate that real estate contributed 5.45% to Nigeria’s GDP in 2024, reflecting a sector that is both resilient and expanding. In the third quarter alone, the market experienced 46.52% nominal growth, aligning with broader global property trends, albeit slightly below the United States’ year-on-year growth of 51.3%. According to Dotun Bamigbola, Senior Partner at Bamigbola Consulting, Nigeria’s estimated population of 230 million and a housing deficit ranging between 20 and 28 million units provide an extensive investment runway. On a continental scale, Africa’s real estate market is projected to reach US$17.64 trillion by 2025, with residential property alone valued at US$14.87 trillion, driven by the expansion of the middle class and infrastructure development. Emerging REIT Market Shows Potential While Nigeria’s Real Estate Investment Trust (REIT) market remains relatively small at approximately $600 million, it demonstrates promise in establishing structured investment vehicles for both local and international investors. By comparison, South Africa’s REIT market stands at $8.5 billion, while Kenya reports $9% average returns on its REIT portfolios. Bamigbola noted that Nigerian REITs currently yield an average return of 7%, highlighting opportunities for operational improvements and structural enhancements. Several REITs, including Union Homes REIT, SFS REIT, and UPDC REIT, continue to demonstrate resilience, collectively generating ₦2.16 billion in rental income in 2023. UPDC REIT: A Case Study in Diversification UPDC REIT exemplifies market diversification, managing over 100 assets spanning commercial, residential, and student accommodation. Key holdings include: Victoria Mall Plaza, Lagos Kingsway Building, Marina UAC House, Abuja Pan-Atlantic University Hostel, Epe Analysts assert that these assets, combined with Nigeria’s substantial housing deficit and rising urbanisation, position the sector as one of the most lucrative investment classes in the country. Investment Outlook Experts highlight that Nigeria’s real estate sector offers a dual opportunity: addressing the national housing deficit while providing robust, long-term returns for investors. Strategic investment in structured instruments, including REITs, and in high-demand residential and commercial properties, can create a resilient and growth-oriented portfolio. The convergence of demographic growth, urban expansion,
summary: Nigeria's real estate market reaching ₦41.3 trillion presents significant investment opportunities, driven by rapid urbanization and housing shortages, which could positively impact Lagos' premium and commercial segments. This growth aligns with Mixta Africa's strategic priorities, particularly in shifting the new-sales mix towards higher-margin segments. The market's resilience and expansion could also enhance the attractiveness of Mixta Africa's projects, such as Lakowe Lakes and Lagos New Town.

mixta_relevance.direct_impact: The growth in Nigeria's real estate market could directly impact Mixta Africa's revenue target for 2026, particularly in the premium and commercial segments.
mixta_relevance.indirect_impact: The market's expansion could lead to increased competition, potentially affecting pricing and costs, but also presents opportunities for strategic partnerships and bulk transactions.
mixta_relevance.strategic_opportunity: The increasing demand for higher-margin segments could create an advantage for Mixta Africa's premium products, such as Lakowe Lakes, and its commercial plots in Lagos New Town.
mixta_relevance.risk_flag: None, as the market growth aligns with Mixta Africa's strategic priorities and presents opportunities for expansion and increased revenue.

title: Africa’s top 10 real estate hotspots to invest in this holiday season
description: Africa’s top 10 real estate hotspots to invest in this holiday season  Business Insider Africa
content: As the year draws to a close and holiday travel surges across the continent, Africa’s real estate market is entering one of its most active investment windows. Africa’s top 10 real estate hotspots to invest in this holiday season Africa's real estate market is rapidly growing due to increased urbanization, infrastructure investment, and global investor interest. The market is projected to reach US$17.64 trillion by 2025, with residential sector leading at US$14.87 trillion. Being physically present in Africa during the holiday season offers diaspora investors a chance to mitigate risks and vet investments firsthand. Key countries for investment include Mauritius, Seychelles, South Africa, and Rwanda, each catering to varied lifestyle and financial preferences. If you’re travelling back to Africa this holiday season, now is an ideal moment to explore real estate investment opportunities. Many in the diaspora often hesitate, concerned about scams, unclear regulations, or being disconnected from local market realities. Being home, even briefly, gives you a unique advantage: you can inspect projects in person, verify titles, and meet trustworthy developers directly, reducing the risks that often discourage overseas investors. ADVERTISEMENT Africa’s real estate market is poised to reach US$17.64 trillion by 2025, driven by rapid urbanization and increased infrastructure investment. According to Statista, the residential sector will lead, with an estimated value of US$14.87 trillion. The market is expected to grow at an annual rate of 5.58% from 2025 to 2029, reaching US$21.92 trillion by the end of the decade. Across the continent, cities are expanding, housing demand is rising, and new policies are opening doors for diaspora investors. Whether you are seeking rental income, long-term capital growth, or a second home, opportunities are stronger than ever and being physically present gives you the edge to act confidently. summary: The article highlights Africa's growing real estate market, which may attract more investors to Lagos, potentially increasing demand for premium and commercial properties, and impacting Mixta Africa's strategic priorities.
